title: artemisia alba subsp. glabrescens
intro: Artemisia alba subsp. glabrescens, also known as White Mugwort or Glabrous Wormwood, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Artemisia alba subsp. glabrescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, menstrual cramps, and anxiety.
